title: bud --base-images
body:
Add a new flag `--base-images` to `buildah bud` which allows for
extracting the base images without performing any build.

Extracting the base images *before* a build is needed in `podman-remote
build` to properly resolve short names prior to performing the build.

Podman will consume the feature via the Go code but exposing it on
the `bud` CLI allows for easier testing.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@nalind @mtrmac @rhatdan PTAL

I'd prefer to just have one search registry for remote podman but since there is the strong desire to have podman-remote behave like the local one, I don't see any other user-friendly option.  The idea for `remote build` is to first extract the base images, then prompt for each where needed (see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11867), and then perform the build.

Why is this a CLI flag rather than a different subcommand?

> Why is this a CLI flag rather than a different subcommand?

It shares large parts of `buildah bud` in the front end, so a flag seemed like a good candidate.  Would hiding the flag work for you?
Closing as we don't pursue remote prompting anymore but solve it by configuring `podman machine` VMs.
